description: The 18th-century artist Jean-Honoré Fragonard is best known for his lighthearted, amorous subjects. This work, painted in lively brushstrokes, represents another important aspect of his genius—his fantasy portraits, in which he referenced earlier artworks to enliven his own characterizations of patrons and artistic friends. In this instance, Fragonard references a painting of an actor by the Italian Baroque artist Domenico Fetti.
title: Portrait of a Man in Costume
